info.title: Oracle REST Data Services (ORDS) REST API
info.description: REST API for managing Oracle REST Data Services (ORDS) RESTful service definitions including modules, templates, handlers, privileges, roles, OAuth clients, and AutoREST-enabled objects. ORDS provides a mid-tier Java application that maps HTTP requests to database transactions, enabling RESTful access to Oracle Database resources. This specification covers the ORDS REST Services Management endpoints available through the Database API stable interface.

port.description: Port number for the ORDS instance

servers.description: ORDS Database API stable endpoint (default port)

tags.name: Modules
tags.description: REST module management. A module is a container for one or more templates with an associated base path.

tags.name: Templates
tags.description: REST module template management. A template defines a URI pattern within a module for routing requests.

tags.name: Handlers
tags.description: REST template handler management. A handler implements an HTTP method on a template with source code.

tags.name: Parameters
tags.description: REST handler parameter management. Parameters define input bindings for handlers.

tags.name: Privileges
tags.description: ORDS privilege management. Privileges control access to REST modules and URL patterns.

tags.name: Roles
tags.description: ORDS role management. Roles group privileges for assignment to users and OAuth clients.

tags.name: OAuth Clients
tags.description: ORDS OAuth client management. OAuth clients enable third-party applications to access protected resources.

tags.name: AutoREST
tags.description: AutoREST object management. AutoREST enables automatic REST access to database tables and views.

tags.name: Overview
tags.description: ORDS schema overview and REST object summaries.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all REST service modules defined in the current schema.

post.description: Creates a new REST service module with the specified name and base path. A module is a container for templates that defines the root URI pattern for a group of related REST services.

200.description: Source code preview of the module creation (when run_mode is codePreview)

404.description: No name or base_path parameter provided

409.description: A module with that name or base path already exists

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific REST service module by its identifier.

put.description: Updates an existing REST service module. All mutable properties of the module can be modified.

409.description: A module with that name or base path already exists

get.description: Retrieves the PL/SQL source code that defines the specified REST service module.
Retrieves a paginated list of all REST module templates defined in the current schema.

post.description: Creates a new REST module template associated with a specific module. A template defines a URI pattern within a module and serves as a container for one or more handlers.

400.description: No pattern parameter provided or module not specified

409.description: A template with that pattern already exists in the module

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific REST module template by its identifier.

put.description: Updates an existing REST module template including its URI pattern, priority, and ETag configuration.

400.description: Missing etag_type, pattern, or priority parameter

409.description: Template pattern already exists in the module

delete.description: Deletes a specific REST module template and all of its associated handlers.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all REST template handlers defined in the current schema.

post.description: Creates a new REST template handler that implements a specific HTTP method on a template. The handler contains the source code (SQL query or PL/SQL block) that will be executed when the template URI is accessed with the specified HTTP method.

409.description: A handler for that method already exists on the template

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific REST template handler by its identifier.

put.description: Updates an existing REST template handler including its source code, source type, and pagination settings.

delete.description: Deletes a specific REST template handler.

get.description: Retrieves the source code implementation of a specific REST template handler.
Retrieves a paginated list of all REST handler parameters defined in the current schema.

200.description: Paginated list of REST handler parameters

post.description: Creates a new parameter binding for a REST template handler, defining how request parameters map to handler source bind variables.

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific REST handler parameter by its identifier.

put.description: Updates an existing REST handler parameter binding.

delete.description: Deletes a specific REST handler parameter.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all ORDS privileges defined in the current schema.

post.description: Creates a new ORDS privilege that controls access to REST modules and URL patterns. Privileges can be associated with roles, patterns, and modules.

409.description: A privilege with that name already exists

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific ORDS privilege by its identifier.

put.description: Updates an existing ORDS privilege including its label, description, and associated roles, patterns, and modules.

get.summary: Get all ORDS roles in a privilege
get.description: Retrieves all roles associated with a specific ORDS privilege.

200.description: List of roles in the privilege

get.summary: Get all REST modules in a privilege
get.description: Retrieves all REST modules protected by a specific ORDS privilege.

200.description: List of modules in the privilege

get.summary: Get all REST patterns in a privilege
get.description: Retrieves all URL patterns protected by a specific ORDS privilege.

200.description: List of patterns in the privilege

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all ORDS roles defined in the current schema.

post.description: Creates a new ORDS role that can be assigned to privileges and OAuth clients to control access to REST services.

409.description: A role with that name already exists

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific ORDS role by its identifier.

get.summary: Get all ORDS privileges for a specific role
get.description: Retrieves all privileges associated with a specific ORDS role.

200.description: List of privileges for the role

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all ORDS OAuth clients registered in the current schema.

post.description: Creates a new ORDS OAuth client that enables third-party applications to access protected REST resources using OAuth 2.0 authorization flows.

409.description: An OAuth client with that name already exists

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific ORDS OAuth client by its identifier.

put.description: Updates an existing ORDS OAuth client configuration.

delete.description: Deletes a specific ORDS OAuth client and revokes all associated tokens.

get.summary: Get all ORDS privileges in an OAuth client
get.description: Retrieves all privileges assigned to a specific ORDS OAuth client.

200.description: List of privileges for the OAuth client

get.summary: Get all ORDS roles in an OAuth client
get.description: Retrieves all roles assigned to a specific ORDS OAuth client.

200.description: List of roles for the OAuth client

post.summary: Update ORDS OAuth client avatar image
post.description: Uploads or updates the avatar image displayed during OAuth authorization for a specific OAuth client.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all database objects that have been REST-enabled through AutoREST in the current schema.

post.description: Enables automatic REST access for a database table or view. AutoREST generates standard CRUD endpoints for the specified object without requiring manual module, template, or handler definitions.

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific REST-enabled object by its identifier.

put.summary: Update or disable a REST enabled object
put.description: Updates the configuration of a REST-enabled object or disables REST access for the object.

get.summary: Get a REST enabled object metadata
get.description: Retrieves the metadata (column definitions, types, constraints) for a REST-enabled database object.

get.summary: Get count of all REST enabled objects
get.description: Retrieves a summary count of all REST-enabled objects in the current schema.
Retrieves all REST modules that are protected by one or more ORDS privileges.

200.description: Paginated list of protected REST modules

get.summary: Get current ORDS schema and REST objects count
get.description: Retrieves an overview of the current schema including counts of REST modules, templates, handlers, privileges, roles, and OAuth clients.

200.description: ORDS schema overview with object counts

get.summary: Get all current schema recent REST objects
get.description: Retrieves the most recently created or modified REST objects in the current schema.
Retrieves all configuration property values for the ORDS instance.
Retrieves the value of a specific ORDS configuration property.

put.description: Sets the value of a specific ORDS configuration property.

delete.description: Removes a custom ORDS configuration property value, reverting it to the default.

get.summary: Get an ORDS property value for a schema
get.description: Retrieves the value of a specific ORDS configuration property scoped to a particular schema.

put.summary: Set an ORDS property value for a schema
put.description: Sets the value of a specific ORDS configuration property scoped to a particular schema.

200.description: Schema-scoped ORDS property value set successfully

delete.summary: Unset an ORDS property value for a schema
delete.description: Removes a schema-scoped ORDS configuration property value, reverting it to the default.

200.description: Schema-scoped ORDS property value unset successfully

basicAuth.description: HTTP Basic authentication using ORDS-enabled database schema credentials

oauth2.description: OAuth 2.0 authorization using ORDS OAuth client credentials or authorization code flow

offset.name: offset
offset.description: The index of the first item to return (0-based). Default is 0.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Maximum number of items to return per page. Default is 25.

LinkRelation.description: HATEOAS link relation describing a related resource or action

rel.description: Link relationship type (e.g., self, describedby, edit, collection)

ResourceItem.description: A single resource item with HATEOAS navigation links. Additional properties are included depending on the resource type.

ItemsCollection.description: Paginated collection of resource items following the ORDS standard collection format

count.description: Total number of records in the current response page

hasMore.description: Indicates whether additional pages of results are available

limit.description: The page size limit applied by the server

offset.description: The starting index of items in this page

items.description: Array of resource items in this page

links.description: Pagination navigation links (first, next, prev, last)

ModuleCreate.description: Request body for creating or updating a REST service module

name.description: Name of the RESTful service module. Case sensitive.

base_path.description: The base URI path used to access this RESTful service. All URIs starting with this path will be serviced by this module.

origins_allowed.description: Comma-separated list of allowed CORS origins for the module

items_per_page.description: Default pagination size for GET handlers (number of rows per page)

run_mode.description: Set to 'codePreview' to return the PL/SQL code that would be executed instead of executing it

TemplateCreate.description: Request body for creating a REST module template

module_id.description: ID of the parent REST module

uri_prefix.description: URI pattern for the template. Supports path parameters using :name syntax and optional parameters using :name? suffix.

priority.description: Priority for evaluating the template when multiple templates match. 0 is lowest, 9 is highest.

etag_type.description: Type of entity tag generation for cache validation and optimistic locking

etag_query.description: SQL query used to generate the entity tag when etag_type is QUERY

run_mode.description: Set to 'codePreview' to return the PL/SQL code instead of executing it

TemplateUpdate.description: Request body for updating a REST module template

etag_query.description: SQL query for entity tag generation when etag_type is QUERY

HandlerCreate.description: Request body for creating or updating a REST template handler

template_id.description: ID of the parent REST template

source.description: The source code implementation (SQL query or PL/SQL block) executed when this handler processes a request

source_type.description: The type of source code that determines how the source is executed

method.description: HTTP method this handler responds to

items_per_page.description: Pagination size for GET handlers. NULL defers to the module-level setting.

mimes_allowed.description: Comma-separated list of MIME types accepted by PUT and POST handlers

run_mode.description: Set to 'codePreview' to return the PL/SQL code instead of executing it

ParameterCreate.description: Request body for creating or updating a REST handler parameter binding

handler_id.description: ID of the parent REST handler

name.description: Name of the parameter as it appears in the request

bind_variable_name.description: Name of the bind variable in the handler source code

PrivilegeCreate.description: Request body for creating or updating an ORDS privilege

name.description: Unique name of the ORDS privilege

roles.description: Null-character-separated list of ORDS roles assigned to this privilege

patterns.description: Null-character-separated list of URL patterns protected by this privilege

modules.description: Null-character-separated list of REST modules protected by this privilege

run_mode.description: Set to 'codePreview' to return the PL/SQL code instead of executing it

RoleCreate.description: Request body for creating or updating an ORDS role

role_name.description: Unique name of the ORDS role

run_mode.description: Set to 'codePreview' to return the PL/SQL code instead of executing it

OAuthClientCreate.description: Request body for creating or updating an ORDS OAuth client

name.description: Unique name for the OAuth client, displayed during the approval phase of three-legged OAuth

privilege_names.description: Comma-separated list of privilege names the client requests access to

description.description: Description of the client purpose, displayed during the OAuth approval phase. Required for three-legged OAuth; may be null for client_credentials grant type.

allowed_origins.description: Comma-separated list of allowed origin URL prefixes for CORS

redirect_uri.description: URI where OAuth redirects with access token or error are sent. Required for three-legged OAuth; may be null for client_credentials grant type.

role_names.description: Comma-separated list of role names assigned to the client

support_email.description: Contact email for end user support

AutoRestEnable.description: Request body for enabling or updating AutoREST on a database object

object_name.description: Name of the database table or view to REST-enable

object_alias.description: Alias used in the REST URI path for the object

auth.description: When true, requires user authorization via ORDS roles before allowing access to the object metadata and data

run_mode.description: Set to 'codePreview' to return the PL/SQL code instead of executing it
